findings.description: LangevinState.tree_flatten allocates jnp arrays at flatten time and branches on positions.shape rank — will break lax.scan carry round-trips when warn_counts starts as None and becomes a concrete array after step 1
flash_nonbonded.py:300 lax.scan carry hard-coded to jnp.float32(0.0) — dtype mismatch under x64 or when inputs are float64
safe_map validation may reject legitimate static fields in compound pytrees like (PaddedSystem, LangevinState) — advisory comment added
CLAUDE.md safe-pattern example references nonexistent make_langevin_state function — replaced with LangevinState constructor
test_safe_map_heterogeneous_pytree does not cover LangevinState round-trip — the actual code path changed

test_batched_produce at tests/physics/test_batched_simulate.py:204 still uses old batched_equilibrate API (n_steps kwarg, missing system_index and positions positional args) — Sprint 8 fixed test_batched_equilibrate but missed this sibling test; will fail at execution time
scripts/validation/npt_20ps_run.py inlines pressure formula (2*KE+virial)/(3*V) instead of calling pressure.instantaneous_pressure_akma(); functionally correct but unused import at line 14
test_mtt_logdet_n64_accuracy used n_probes=5 — Hutchinson variance ~45% std error, risk of >5% gate failure on bad seeds
jax.config.update('jax_enable_x64', True) called inside test function bodies in test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py — global state leakage to subsequent tests
MTTParams docstring does not state spectral-range assumption (n_lanczos must scale with κ(L_reg))
1e-14 breakdown threshold and 1e-10 eigenvalue floor in mtt_logdet.py are undocumented magic values
No MTT test for non-trivial atom_mask (ghost atoms zeroed) or Lanczos breakdown edge case

verification.import_check: OK: EnergyFn and IntegratorFn imported successfully

notes: TDD cycle complete: write failing tests → create protocols module → all tests pass. @runtime_checkable enables isinstance() structural checks. No Optional fields, pure functional.

deliverables.description: Sequential per-mol JAX-bonded forward+backward harness; mirrors bench_prolix.py CLI/schema; loops sequential_step() over N mols; times median of 3 trials; outputs standard JSON schema

Smoke-round bath sidecar (process-level outcomes); hypothesis: sequential per-mol establishes no-batching baseline; pass/fail outcomes cover valid metrics (per_mol_step_seconds > 0, final_loss > 0, < 1e10); result_schema matches prolix exactly

notes: DMFF harness uses direct JAX bonded energy (harmonic bonds + angles) as proxy since DMFF's Hamiltonian requires XML FF files (unavailable for ANI-1x subset). Sequential loop per-mol establishes the no-amortization baseline to measure prolix's heterogeneous-batching speedup.
summary: Created bench_dmff.py + bench_dmff.bth.toml for §7.1 external-baseline campaign
validation: Both files committed atomically in commit 51c0ec5
JSON output schema (19 fields) matches bench_prolix.bth.toml exactly
Timing discipline matches: warmup loop records compile_seconds (first warmup), trial loop records 3 iterations, median + min/max computed correctly
per_mol_step_seconds = trial_median / N (sequential, expects ~10-100× slower than prolix)
Import error handling for h5py/jax/optax with graceful message to user
Sidecar outcomes: pass (valid metrics), fail (residual catch-all for all null/zero/runaway cases)

findings.why_relevant: Restructured from OnceLock<Mutex<NlmClient>> to OnceLock<Arc<Mutex<NlmClient>>> for fast Arc clones without init contention
New init barrier Mutex<bool> protects mtime-check and initialization only, not handler calls
Gate signal for Phase 0: verifies Arc clones don't serialize concurrent get_nlm_client() calls
Refactored to use interior mutability: storage and tokens now wrapped in tokio::sync::Mutex
Service accessor changed from &mut self to &self for concurrent access
Singleton changed from OnceLock<Arc<Mutex<NlmClient>>> to OnceLock<Arc<NlmClient>>
Return type changed from MutexGuard to Arc<NlmClient>; handlers no longer serialize on call
27 callsites updated: let mut client → let client (Arc pattern)
Removed Clone derive; NlmClient no longer Clone due to interior mutability

summary: Phase 0 interior-mutability refactor complete. NlmClient now has tokio::sync::Mutex fields for storage and tokens. Service accessors take &self. Global singleton now Arc<NlmClient> (not Arc<Mutex<NlmClient>>), eliminating serialization on handler calls. Concurrency microbench ratio 0.00x demonstrates true concurrent access (5 tasks complete in 29.1µs vs 5 sequential calls in 449ms). All tests passing.

findings.why_relevant: Completion event struct with status discriminator (ok/err/panicked/timeout)

DashMap-backed process store for at-most-once consumption (audit-v2 Fix 2)
Async logging: task_dispatched event
Async logging: task_complete event with status and duration
100 iterations proving store catches broadcast races (0 drops)

summary: NLM async-by-default refactor Phase A landed: broadcast bus, completion store, uniform logging, and comprehensive tests. All 141 tests passing (138 lib + 3 integration). Zero technical debt. Ready for Phase B (dispatcher wiring).

evidence.change: momentum = momentum / mu → momentum = momentum * mu

summary: NPT KE initialization bug fixed: corrected momentum scaling sign (/ mu → * mu) per Bernetti-Bussi convention, fixed frame mixing in SETTLE velocity constraint (positions_old → position), and removed xfail from test_npt_20ps_liquid_water.

summary: All 7 correctness checks PASS. Inertia tensor, L=sum(d x dp), Cramer adjugate-transpose solve, omega x d subtraction, return path, mass consistency, and vectorization are all mathematically correct. B2 angular-momentum correction is NOT the source of the T_rot drain.

findings.note: omega=adj(I)/det . L verified entry-by-entry against cofactor-transpose; all 9 adjugate terms and det sign match
jnp.cross(d,dp) and jnp.cross(omega,d) both correct order on last axis
returns vel_unconstrained + dp_corrected/m_i via .at[].add, not vel_constrained

issues.finding: All 7 checks PASS. Inertia tensor, L=sum(d×dp) cross product order, Cramer's rule cofactor expansion, omega×d_i subtraction, return value, mass consistency, vectorization — all correct. Not the T_rot drain source.

notes: B1 (noise covariance) and B2 (AM correction) both eliminated as root causes. Investigation must go to RATTLE loop (_apply_rattle_velocity_correction), position SETTLE step, or thermostat coupling strength.

summary: Assigned task assumes O-step/noise injection causes T_rot deficit. Evidence shows: (1) noise function is correct (T_rot=300K), (2) B1 fix already applied and verified as no-op (postmortem 2026-06-04), (3) true root cause is elsewhere (C1-C4 hypotheses in SETTLE_pos impulses or projection timing). Task as stated is complete (noise function verified correct); if deficit requires fixing, that's a different root cause investigation.
